title: Normal distribution
short: A symmetric bell-shaped distribution described by its mean μ and standard deviation σ.
full:
The normal (Gaussian) distribution is the familiar symmetric bell curve, fully described by its mean μ (the centre) and standard deviation σ (the spread). Many natural measurements — heights, errors, test scores — are approximately normal.

The empirical (68–95–99.7) rule says about 68% of data lie within 1σ of the mean, 95% within 2σ, and 99.7% within 3σ. To find probabilities, convert a value to a z-score and read the standard normal table.
